description:
Rolling build performance baselines for orionrobots.github.io.

This file is maintained automatically. After each successful push to master the
on_push_update_baseline workflow runs both smoke tests, records the raw metrics
here, and commits the result with '[skip ci]' so no build loop is triggered.

Metrics recorded per entry:
  webpack.buildTimeMs     — webpack production bundle build time in milliseconds
  webpack.bundleSizeBytes — size of dist/bundle.js in bytes
  eleventy.buildTimeMs    — Eleventy full-site build time in milliseconds
  eleventy.siteSizeBytes  — total size of the _site output directory in bytes
  eleventy.htmlFileCount  — number of HTML files generated in _site

How baselines are used in PRs:
  The rolling average of all stored entries is computed for each metric and shown
  alongside the current PR run value. A percentage delta is displayed and colour-
  coded so reviewers can immediately tell whether a change is within normal noise
  (🟢 ≤5%), worth noting (🟡 5–20%), or a significant regression/improvement
  (🔴 >20%).

Retention:
  Only the most recent 'maxBaselines' entries are kept. Older entries are dropped
  when a new one is appended, giving a rolling window rather than unbounded growth.
  Four entries is enough to smooth out single-run noise while staying responsive
  to genuine trends.
